calendar.matters:
Notice Of Defendant Delta Dental Of California'S Application For Relief Pursuant To Ccp 473(B) (Applications)
Off Calendar Jun-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
JASON M. ERLICH, ESQ., ERLICH LAW FIRM, P.C., (510) 390-9140, jason@erlichlawfirm.com, for Plaintiff KAMELIA BEGLEY,
ANNIE LAU, ESQ., FISHER & PHILLIPS LLP, (415) 490-9000 alau@fisherphillips.com for Defendant DELTA DENTAL.

Not Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S Application For Relief Pursuant To Ccp 473(B).

Defendant's motion for relief is granted to the extent that it is even necessary. Defendant filed a timely appellate costs memorandum but served it at the wrong address. Defendant's failure to serve the appellate costs memorandum at the correct address was the result of mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect within the meaning of CCP 473. Trial counsel was reasonably unaware that plaintiff's counsel's address changed during the course of the appeal because other counsel litigated the appeal. (Lau Decl.) The court notes that there is no prejudice to plaintiff since the court addresses the merits of the costs issue in the companion motion to tax/strike.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Defendant Memorandum Of Costs On Appeal
Off Calendar Jun-08-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
JASON M. ERLICH, ESQ., ERLICH LAW FIRM, P.C., (510) 390-9140, jason@erlichlawfirm.com, for Plaintiff KAMELIA BEGLEY,
ANNIE LAU, ESQ., FISHER & PHILLIPS LLP, (415) 490-9000 alau@fisherphillips.com for Defendant DELTA DENTAL.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAMELIA BEGLEY'S Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative Tax Defendant Memorandum Of Costs On Appeal.

Plaintiff's motion to strike/tax defendant's appellate costs memorandum and to vacate this court's order of March 2, 2022 (entered on March 3, 2022) affirming those costs is denied.

The Court of Appeal opinion states: "Respondents are entitled to costs on appeal." (Lau Decl. 5, Exhibit D at p. 19.) This court cannot overrule that determination. If plaintiff believed that that ruling was erroneous, it was incumbent upon her to seek relief from the Court of Appeal before the remittitur issued. When an appellate court directs in its opinion a party is to recover costs on appeal, neither the lower court nor the unsuccessful party is entitled to overrule the appellate court. (See Ramirez v. St. Paul Fire and Marine Insurance Company (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 473, 478-479; In re Marriage of Jovel (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 575, 590-591.) An unsuccessful party who is dissatisfied with the costs award should seek relief in the appellate court before the remittitur is issued. (Ramirez, 35 Cal.App.4th at 478.)

As explained in the companion motion, there is good cause to grant relief for defendant's service of the appellate costs memorandum at the wrong address. The court further notes that there is no prejudice since this motion addresses the merits of the costs award.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of And Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative, Tax Defendants Memorandum Of Costs;Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jason Erlich, Esq. of MCCORMACK & ERLICH, T:415.296.8420, for plaintiff/moving party;
Annie Lau, Esq. of FISHER PHILLIPS LLP, T:415.490.9023, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The Court's tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF KAMELIA BEGLEY's Motion To Strike, Or In The Alternative, Tax Defendants Memorandum Of Costs, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to strike defendant's memorandum of costs is granted. In a FEHA action, a "prevailing defendant . . . should not be awarded fees and costs unless the court finds the action was objectively without foundation when brought, or the plaintiff continued to litigate after it clearly became so." (Williams v. Chino Valley Indep. Fire Dist. (2015) 61 Cal.4th 97, 115.) Similarly, costs following a Code of Civ. Proc. 998 offer are allowable in FEHA actions only when "the court finds the action was frivolous, unreasonable, or groundless." (Gov. Code 12965(b).)

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Plaintiff's motion is granted to the extent that defendant shall revise the subpoena scope as agreed at the hearing. Plaintiff shall prepare a proposed order and submit it to the Judge Pro Tem.

Motion To Compel Further Responses And To Produce Documents
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Erlich, Esq. 415-296-8420 of McCormack & Erlich for Plaintiff.
Aaron Langberg, Esq. 415-490-9000 of Fisher Philips for Defendant Delta Dental.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAMELIA BEGLEY's Motion To Compel Further Responses And To Produce Documents.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification.

The Motion is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. First, the Court finds that the Motion as to RFPs 7 and 39 is timely. Defendant's unverified "First Supplemental Response to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents and Electronically Stored Information, Set One" was personally served on May 7, 2019. The responses appear on their face to amend RFPs 7 and 39. The verification was served on May 8, 2019 via mail. 50 days after May 8, 2019 is June 27, 2019. The Motion was filed on June 25, 2019, within 45 days of the responses. (Erlich Decl., Ex. I).

Second, the Court finds that plaintiff's counsel adequately met and conferred with defense counsel. The Court makes the following specific rulings:

7: The Motion is GRANTED, all objections are overruled.

10: The Motion as to RFP #10 is denied as moot. (See Reply 1:10-12)

18: The Motion is DENIED, defendant provided an adequate response and evidence that all documents have been provided. (Osmani Decl., par. 3-6).

39: The Motion is GRANTED, all objections are overruled.

The Court further finds that the documents requested are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The Court finds that the separate statement is sufficiently code-complaint. Amended, verified responses and responsive documents (if applicable) are due within one week of notice of this order. If amended, verified responses and responsive documents (if applicable) are not served within one week of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $2,500 are due and payable against defendant and its counsel of record in favor of plaintiff within two weeks of notice of entry of this order.

Additionally, the scope of RFP #39 is limited to 3/1/18-3/1/19.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
